readability.details: Function header comment explains rename strategy, error handling pattern, and generation shift order. All code blocks have inline comments. Variable names (file_size_bytes, max_size_bytes, MAX_LOG_SIZE_MB, MAX_LOG_GENERATIONS) are self-documenting.

shell_best_practices.details: set -e at script top. All variables double-quoted. POSIX-compliant file size check (wc -c < file). local variables used in function. Arithmetic via $((...)) not deprecated expr. while loop with proper decrement.

style_consistency.details: Follows existing script conventions: same header comment format as stop-server.sh, same PORT validation pattern with [S4-001] tag, same lsof pipeline with grep/sort, same PID file validation (head -1 | grep -E). Echo messages use === prefix matching existing build-and-start.sh patterns.

security_tags.details: [S4-001] port validation, [S4-003] PID file chmod 600, [S4-005] log file chmod 640, [S4-006] symlink guard - all tags present and correctly placed at their respective code sites.

test_alignment.details: All 11 tests pass. Tests cover: function existence, constants, call site pattern, chmod 640, call positioning, no-file early return, under-threshold skip, basic rotation, generation shift, symlink guard for LOG_FILE, symlink guard for generation files.

shellcheck_notes: shellcheck reports 2 info-level SC2086 warnings for unquoted $PORT and $PORT_PIDS in lsof/echo commands. Both are safe: PORT is validated numeric-only before use, PORT_PIDS is filtered through grep -E '^[0-9]+$'. The unquoted pattern is consistent with stop-server.sh, stop.sh, health-check.sh, and status.sh in the same codebase. No change made to preserve style consistency.
message: Code review complete. The rotate_logs() implementation is well-structured, follows shell scripting best practices, maintains style consistency with the existing codebase, has proper security tags, and aligns with all 11 test expectations. No refactoring changes needed.
